BLOCKER: packages/sqlalchemy/src is entirely absent from scripts/check_quality.py's FORMAT_LINT_PATHS and TYPING_PATHS, and absent from root pyproject.toml's [tool.mypy] mypy_path. It is also absent from sys.check_quality.py's code_quality_report.py --path argument list. Verified by direct execution: running `ruff check packages/sqlalchemy/src` passes cleanly, but `mypy packages/sqlalchemy/src` independently produces 17 errors across 9 files (missing py.typed marker on every internal cross-module import, plus one genuine bug: db/stores/video_action_set.py:1070 `Result[Any]` has no attribute `rowcount`). None of this is caught by any CI gate today - it is silently excluded, not silently passing.

Same gap as sqlalchemy/src: absent from every quality tool invocation. The sqlalchemy-package.yml workflow does separately run ruff format/check and mypy on packages/sqlalchemy/src,tests as one of its own steps (windows-latest only) - so sqlalchemy DOES get ruff/mypy coverage via that dedicated workflow, but NOT via the repository-wide check_quality.py gate used by python.yml and package-integration.yml. This is an inconsistency between the repo-wide gate and the per-package gate, not a total absence of coverage.

scripts/check_architecture.py hardcodes PACKAGE_ROOT = REPO_ROOT / 'zeromodel' (line 10), the deleted root distribution directory. Verified empirically in a clean git worktree at baseline SHA c1ce710: root zeromodel/ does not exist, discover_modules() finds 0 modules, and the script prints 'Architecture check: passed' having checked nothing. This is the 'Architecture' step inside check_quality.py (called by python.yml's quality job and by package-integration.yml's Quality gate step) - it is a silent no-op on every invocation post-split. Note this is a DIFFERENT script from scripts/check_package_boundaries.py, which correctly reads package-boundaries.toml's packages/*/src source_root entries and DOES work (verified: 'Package boundary check passed: 112 production modules' in the same clean worktree) - but check_package_boundaries.py is only invoked directly by the six per-package workflows, never by check_quality.py itself.

14 of 35 legacy_exceptions entries (40%) reference file paths that no longer exist in the repo (e.g. zeromodel/video_action_set_benchmark.py, zeromodel/db/stores/video_action_set.py, tests/test_video_split_progress_kernel.py which actually now lives at research/video_action_set/tests/). These are inert (code_quality_report.py simply never matches them to a real file) but indicate the baseline was never reconciled after the split. Verified by loading the TOML and checking Path.exists() for every legacy_exceptions key in a clean worktree.

scripts/check_package_boundaries.py does `import tomllib` unconditionally with no try/except fallback (unlike scripts/validate_release_candidate.py, which falls back to `import tomli`). tomllib is Python-3.11+ stdlib only. Every packages/*/pyproject.toml declares requires-python >= 3.10, and requirements-dev.txt is meant to support that floor, but requirements-dev.txt does not list tomli. check_package_boundaries.py would raise ModuleNotFoundError on a Python 3.10 dev environment. This does not currently surface in CI because every workflow that calls check_package_boundaries.py (the six per-package workflows) pins Python 3.12 only - but it is a real gap for anyone following the documented Python-3.10-compatible development install.
